events.seo.title: Qesto for Events | Live polling for conferences
events.seo.description: Run live polls, ranked Q&A, and sentiment checks across keynote and breakout, then deliver sponsor-ready engagement evidence by session.
events.headline: Turn every keynote into a conversation.
events.subheadline: Run live polls, Q&A rankings, and sentiment checks across hundreds of participants — Qesto keeps your audience engaged from opening remarks to closing session.
events.imageAlt: Speaker presenting at a conference with engaged audience
events.painPoints.heading: The challenges every event organiser knows
events.painPoints.passive.title: Audience attention drops invisibly
events.painPoints.passive.desc: Engagement falls 20 minutes into a one-way session and the data shows up after the room has emptied.
events.painPoints.feedback.title: Post-event surveys arrive too late
events.painPoints.feedback.desc: By the time the recap survey lands, the speaker is gone and next year's brief is already being written.
events.painPoints.silent.title: Sponsor signal stays hidden
events.painPoints.silent.desc: Only confident voices reach the microphone. The signals sponsors actually paid for never make it into the recap.
events.features.heading: Everything you need to run a live event people remember
events.features.subheading: One join code captures engagement evidence from opening keynote through the closing panel.
events.features.livePolls.title: Live polls
events.features.livePolls.desc: Capture sentiment and comprehension in seconds; project results behind the speaker in the same heartbeat.
events.features.ranking.title: Ranked Q&A
events.features.ranking.desc: Attendees upvote questions in real time so speakers spend stage time on what the room actually wants.
events.features.openQuestions.title: Open response themes
events.features.openQuestions.desc: Collect free-text feedback and let edge-AI clustering surface themes for the recap and the sponsor report.
events.features.consent.title: Consent rounds
events.features.consent.desc: Run structured agreement checks before sensitive panels, community decisions, or invite-only sessions.
events.proof.heading: What event teams report after switching
events.proof.metric1.label: Live participation
events.proof.metric1.note: Illustrative benchmark from moderated event formats using show-of-hands baselines.
events.proof.metric2.label: To launch the first poll
events.proof.metric2.note: Illustrative benchmark when hosts pre-stage the first poll before session start.
events.proof.metric3.label: Sponsor evidence
events.proof.metric3.note: Per-session engagement records sponsors can include directly in recap reporting.
events.proof.testimonial.quote: We stopped guessing audience energy. Every speaker now has live data to adjust their talk inside the session.
events.proof.testimonial.author: Programme Director
events.scenarios.heading: How event teams use Qesto
events.scenarios.warmup.title: Opening-keynote warm-up
events.scenarios.warmup.desc: Open with a single high-signal question so the speaker baselines audience context inside the first three minutes.
events.scenarios.qa.title: Ranked speaker Q&A
events.scenarios.qa.desc: Collect and rank questions live so presenters spend their last fifteen minutes on what the room rated highest.
events.scenarios.pulse.title: Sponsored-session pulse
events.scenarios.pulse.desc: Run end-of-session pulses tied to a sponsor track so the post-event recap carries hard engagement evidence.
events.related.heading: Related pages
events.related.link1.label: Feature: Live Polling
events.related.link1.desc: Polls, rankings, and consent rounds inside one session.
events.related.link2.label: Feature: AI Insights
events.related.link2.desc: Turn open feedback into themes for sponsor-ready recaps.
events.related.link3.label: Use case: Workshops
events.related.link3.desc: Run sponsor or breakout workshops with structured input.
events.faq.heading: Common event-team questions
events.faq.q1.question: Can attendees join without creating an account?
events.faq.q1.answer: Yes. Participants join with a short code in any modern browser. No account, no install.
events.faq.q2.question: Does this work for hybrid and remote attendees?
events.faq.q2.answer: Yes. In-room and remote participants use the same join code and follow the same question flow at the same time.
events.faq.q3.question: Can we hand sponsor-ready data to partners?
events.faq.q3.answer: Yes. Per-session engagement and theme outputs export to CSV or JSON suitable for sponsor recap decks.
events.bottomCta.heading: Your next event starts here.
events.bottomCta.subheading: Set up in under 5 minutes, no app install required for attendees.
hr.seo.title: Qesto for HR | Anonymous pulse surveys with edge AI
hr.seo.description: Run trust-first pulse sessions with three anonymity modes, AI theme detection on Cloudflare's edge, and consent logs your People team can audit.
hr.headline: Feel the pulse of your people — not just the data.
hr.subheadline: Run anonymous pulse surveys, onboarding check-ins, and engagement sessions that employees actually respond to. AI surfaces what your team is really thinking.
hr.imageAlt: HR team in a collaborative discussion
hr.painPoints.heading: The gaps annual surveys can't fill
hr.painPoints.stale.title: Sentiment data arrives too late
hr.painPoints.stale.desc: Quarterly engagement surveys surface friction long after the right manager could have acted on it.
hr.painPoints.fear.title: Employees doubt anonymity
hr.painPoints.fear.desc: If the anonymity model is implicit or hidden, participation drops and the answers you do get are sanitized.
hr.painPoints.fatigue.title: Survey fatigue erodes trust
hr.painPoints.fatigue.desc: Long forms with no visible follow-through teach employees the next survey isn't worth opening.
hr.features.heading: A better way to listen at scale
hr.features.anonymity.title: Three anonymity modes per session
hr.features.anonymity.desc: Pick full anonymity, cohort grouping, or identified responses. The active mode is shown to participants before they answer.
hr.features.pulse.title: Fast pulse templates
hr.features.pulse.desc: Launch focused check-ins after all-hands moments, manager forums, and 30/60/90 onboarding milestones in minutes.
hr.features.ai.title: AI theme detection on the edge
hr.features.ai.desc: Workers AI clusters open responses inside Cloudflare's edge network — employee text never reaches third-party model providers.
hr.features.gdpr.title: Audit-ready consent log
hr.features.gdpr.desc: Time-stamped consent records suitable for DSAR responses and internal People-data review.
hr.proof.heading: Outcomes HR teams target
hr.proof.metric1.label: Pulse response rate
hr.proof.metric1.note: Illustrative target for short weekly sessions replacing quarterly long-form surveys.
hr.proof.metric2.label: Listening cadence
hr.proof.metric2.note: Sustained with one to three questions and visible follow-through.
hr.proof.metric3.label: Theme-summary turnaround
hr.proof.metric3.note: Illustrative target for first People-team review after session close.
hr.proof.testimonial.quote: Participation moved once people could see which anonymity mode was active before they answered.
hr.proof.testimonial.author: Head of People
hr.scenarios.heading: How people teams use Qesto
hr.scenarios.allHands.title: All-hands pulse
hr.scenarios.allHands.desc: Capture confidence and clarity within the hour after a leadership update — before the rumor mill writes the recap.
hr.scenarios.onboarding.title: 30/60/90 onboarding check-ins
hr.scenarios.onboarding.desc: Spot friction themes by cohort and feed them into your manager onboarding playbook each month.
hr.scenarios.manager.title: Manager effectiveness loops
hr.scenarios.manager.desc: Collect upward feedback in a fully anonymous mode and equip managers with theme-level coaching data.
hr.related.heading: Related pages
hr.related.link1.label: Feature: Privacy
hr.related.link1.desc: Review the anonymity modes and consent log in detail.
hr.related.link2.label: Feature: AI Insights
hr.related.link2.desc: See how open feedback becomes editable themes on the edge.
hr.related.link3.label: Use case: Team Meetings
hr.related.link3.desc: Bring pulse-style check-ins into regular team cadences.
hr.faq.heading: Common HR questions
hr.faq.q1.question: Can we run fully anonymous sessions?
hr.faq.q1.answer: Yes. Full anonymity is one of three modes (full, cohort, identified) and the active mode is shown to participants before they answer.
hr.faq.q2.question: How do managers consume the results?
hr.faq.q2.answer: Managers receive theme-level summaries and quantitative trends suitable for action planning, not raw comment dumps.
hr.faq.q3.question: Do we need an HRIS integration to start?
hr.faq.q3.answer: No. Most teams begin without one and decide on integrations once the listening cadence is established.
hr.bottomCta.heading: Build a culture where everyone has a voice.
hr.bottomCta.subheading: Start met Pulse — geen HR-software-integratie vereist.
nonprofit.seo.title: Qesto for Nonprofits | Consent voting and audit logs
nonprofit.seo.description: Run inclusive board votes, member consultations, and program prioritization with consent voting, anonymity options, and verifiable decision evidence.
nonprofit.headline: Give every member a voice that counts.
nonprofit.subheadline: Run transparent votes, consent checks, and community feedback sessions that keep your members engaged and your board accountable — all on a privacy-first platform.
nonprofit.imageAlt: Community group in a collaborative session
nonprofit.painPoints.heading: What gets in the way of real community voice
nonprofit.painPoints.attendance.title: Members are excluded by geography
nonprofit.painPoints.attendance.desc: Members who cannot attend in person are routinely left out of the decisions that matter most to them.
nonprofit.painPoints.opaque.title: Outcomes feel manual and opaque
nonprofit.painPoints.opaque.desc: Show-of-hands and paper ballots leave too much room for dispute about how the result was reached.
nonprofit.painPoints.loudest.title: Loudest voices set the room
nonprofit.painPoints.loudest.desc: Public discussion formats suppress honest input from quieter members and minority positions.
nonprofit.features.heading: Tools built for transparent, inclusive decisions
nonprofit.features.consent.title: Structured consent voting
nonprofit.features.consent.desc: Capture support, abstentions, and reasoned objections — not just majority counts — so dissent has a place to land.
nonprofit.features.anonymous.title: Anonymous input options
nonprofit.features.anonymous.desc: Run sensitive questions in full anonymity while keeping turnout figures verifiable.
nonprofit.features.languages.title: Multilingual participation
nonprofit.features.languages.desc: Member-facing UI in English, Dutch, German, French, and Spanish — useful for cross-border memberships.
nonprofit.features.audit.title: Verifiable decision evidence
nonprofit.features.audit.desc: Each session produces an exportable record (turnout, mode, results, objections) suitable for AGM minutes and board review.
nonprofit.proof.heading: Governance outcomes nonprofits target
nonprofit.proof.metric1.label: Remote member turnout
nonprofit.proof.metric1.note: Illustrative target when short-code voting is available across mobile and desktop.
nonprofit.proof.metric2.label: Vote-to-rationale trail
nonprofit.proof.metric2.note: Outcome, mode, turnout, and objections remain in one exportable artifact.
nonprofit.proof.metric3.label: AGM minute draft prep
nonprofit.proof.metric3.note: Illustrative target using structured exports instead of manual spreadsheet reconciliation.
nonprofit.proof.testimonial.quote: We moved from contested outcomes to a process members trust — even when they disagreed with the result.
nonprofit.proof.testimonial.author: Board Secretary
nonprofit.scenarios.heading: How nonprofits and communities use Qesto
nonprofit.scenarios.agm.title: AGM and board voting
nonprofit.scenarios.agm.desc: Run live or asynchronous member votes with a verifiable record suitable for minutes and audit.
nonprofit.scenarios.priorities.title: Program prioritization
nonprofit.scenarios.priorities.desc: Rank proposed initiatives before budget conversations so funding follows member intent.
nonprofit.scenarios.volunteer.title: Volunteer feedback loops
nonprofit.scenarios.volunteer.desc: Capture post-event input from volunteers and surface theme-level patterns coordinators can act on.
nonprofit.related.heading: Related pages
nonprofit.related.link1.label: Feature: Privacy
nonprofit.related.link1.desc: Review anonymity options and consent log behavior.
nonprofit.related.link2.label: Feature: Live Polling
nonprofit.related.link2.desc: See voting and ranking formats available for member sessions.
nonprofit.related.link3.label: Use case: Workshops
nonprofit.related.link3.desc: Run member workshops with structured facilitation and shareable outputs.
nonprofit.faq.heading: Common nonprofit questions
nonprofit.faq.q1.question: Can members vote remotely?
nonprofit.faq.q1.answer: Yes. Members join with a short code from any browser-enabled device, including mobile.
nonprofit.faq.q2.question: Can we keep a verifiable trail of the vote?
nonprofit.faq.q2.answer: Yes. Each session retains turnout, mode, results, and recorded objections — all exportable for governance review.
nonprofit.faq.q3.question: Do we have to use identified voting?
nonprofit.faq.q3.answer: No. You choose the anonymity mode per session: full anonymity, cohort grouping, or identified.
nonprofit.bottomCta.heading: Democracy starts with being heard.
nonprofit.bottomCta.subheading: Pulse is gratis. Geen creditcard vereist.
consulting.seo.title: Qesto for Consulting | Workshops with same-day evidence
consulting.seo.description: Replace sticky notes and manual synthesis with workshop workflows that leave clients with same-day decision evidence and reusable templates.
consulting.headline: Workshops that actually move the room.
consulting.subheadline: Replace sticky notes and show-of-hands with structured real-time data. Qesto helps facilitators surface alignment, expose hidden disagreements, and close workshops with clear decisions.
consulting.imageAlt: Facilitator leading a collaborative workshop session
consulting.painPoints.heading: What slows workshops down
consulting.painPoints.consensus.title: Verbal agreement masks real dissent
consulting.painPoints.consensus.desc: Heads nod in the room and the objection arrives in an email three days later — usually from the sponsor.
consulting.painPoints.debrief.title: Clients receive photos, not deliverables
consulting.painPoints.debrief.desc: Whiteboard photos and post-it transcriptions don't survive the handoff to the team that has to execute.
consulting.painPoints.logistics.title: Manual synthesis eats facilitation time
consulting.painPoints.logistics.desc: Affinity-clustering and ranking by hand consumes the hours you should be spending on the next conversation.
consulting.features.heading: Every tool a facilitator needs, in one session link
consulting.features.ranking.title: Priority ranking
consulting.features.ranking.desc: Show what the group actually values most with transparent live ranking — no facilitator-influence dot vote.
consulting.features.consent.title: Consent rounds
consulting.features.consent.desc: Test decision readiness and surface objections before close, so the email three days later is unnecessary.
consulting.features.open.title: Anonymous open input
consulting.features.open.desc: Capture candid perspectives in full anonymity and let edge AI cluster them into themes for the debrief slide.
consulting.features.export.title: Same-day deliverable export
consulting.features.export.desc: Structured session outputs export to slide-ready summaries clients can share before they leave the building.
consulting.proof.heading: What facilitation teams optimize for
consulting.proof.metric1.label: Synthesis overhead
consulting.proof.metric1.note: Illustrative benchmark versus sticky-note and manual transcription workflows.
consulting.proof.metric2.label: Client recap delivery
consulting.proof.metric2.note: Structured decisions and rationale can be shared before participants leave the room.
consulting.proof.metric3.label: Engagement templates
consulting.proof.metric3.note: Standardize a facilitation flow once and adapt per client.
consulting.proof.testimonial.quote: Clients leave with a decision and the evidence behind it — not a folder of photos and a promise of slides next week.
consulting.proof.testimonial.author: Lead Facilitator
consulting.scenarios.heading: Facilitation formats that use Qesto
consulting.scenarios.strategy.title: Strategy alignment
consulting.scenarios.strategy.desc: Rank strategic options and run a consent round before signing off on direction.
consulting.scenarios.retro.title: Distributed retrospectives
consulting.scenarios.retro.desc: Collect asynchronous input ahead of the call, then debrief clustered themes live.
consulting.scenarios.design.title: Design-thinking workshops
consulting.scenarios.design.desc: Move from divergent ideation to convergent prioritization with the evidence trail intact.
consulting.related.heading: Related pages
consulting.related.link1.label: Use case: Workshops
consulting.related.link1.desc: See the facilitation craft view of the same workflow.
consulting.related.link2.label: Feature: AI Insights
consulting.related.link2.desc: Cut synthesis from a half day to one debrief meeting.
consulting.related.link3.label: Feature: Live Polling
consulting.related.link3.desc: Run high-velocity ranking and consent rounds in the room.
consulting.faq.heading: Common consulting questions
consulting.faq.q1.question: Can I reuse workshop templates across clients?
consulting.faq.q1.answer: Yes. Standardize a facilitation flow once and adapt question wording per engagement — the structure travels.
consulting.faq.q2.question: Does it work for hybrid client sessions?
consulting.faq.q2.answer: Yes. In-room and remote participants share the same join code and the same question flow at the same time.
consulting.faq.q3.question: Can I deliver client-ready outputs the same day?
consulting.faq.q3.answer: Yes. Session outputs export to structured summaries you can share before participants leave the room.
consulting.bottomCta.heading: Run better workshops, close with data.
consulting.bottomCta.subheading: Set up your first session in under 5 minutes.
features.aiInsights.seo.title: AI Insights | Question generation and theme detection
features.aiInsights.seo.description: Draft a session question set in under 90 seconds and cluster open responses into themes — without sending participant data to third-party LLMs.
features.aiInsights.headline: AI Insights — questions written in seconds, patterns found automatically.
features.aiInsights.subheadline: Qesto's on-device AI generates question sets from a brief, detects themes across sessions, and surfaces what your team is really thinking — without a single byte leaving your infrastructure.
features.aiInsights.howItWorks.heading: How AI Insights works
features.aiInsights.howItWorks.step1.tit: Describe the session in one line
features.aiInsights.howItWorks.step1.des: Paste the session goal and audience. Workers AI returns a draft question flow you can edit, reorder, or replace before going live.
features.aiInsights.howItWorks.step2.des: Capture polls, rankings, consent rounds, and open responses in real time with the anonymity mode you chose for the session.
features.aiInsights.howItWorks.step3.des: Open responses are grouped into themes after the session closes. Themes are editable — your facilitator decides what ships in the recap.
features.aiInsights.outcomes.heading: What teams experience
features.aiInsights.outcomes.item1.desc: Typical time from a one-line brief to an editable first-draft question set.
features.aiInsights.outcomes.item2.desc: Workers AI inference runs inside the same network as your session — no third-party LLM calls.
features.aiInsights.outcomes.item3.desc: Open responses are grouped so debriefs start from patterns, not from raw comment dumps.
features.aiInsights.proof.heading: What AI-assisted hosts measure
features.aiInsights.proof.metric1.label: Brief-to-draft turnaround
features.aiInsights.proof.metric1.note: Measured for a 5–7 question session brief.
features.aiInsights.proof.metric2.label: Third-party model calls
features.aiInsights.proof.metric2.note: Inference is served by Cloudflare Workers AI.
features.aiInsights.proof.metric3.label: Themes before publish
features.aiInsights.proof.metric3.note: Hosts approve theme labels before they appear in any recap.
features.aiInsights.related.heading: See AI Insights in context
features.aiInsights.related.link1.label: Qesto for HR
features.aiInsights.related.link1.desc: Use themes to summarize sensitive employee feedback safely.
features.aiInsights.related.link2.label: Qesto for Consulting
features.aiInsights.related.link2.desc: Cut workshop synthesis from a half day to the recap meeting.
features.aiInsights.related.link3.label: Use case: Workshops
features.aiInsights.related.link3.desc: Apply AI clustering to design-thinking and prioritization rounds.
features.aiInsights.faq.heading: Common AI Insights questions
features.aiInsights.faq.q1.question: Can I edit AI-generated questions?
features.aiInsights.faq.q1.answer: Yes. Drafts are fully editable before the session starts and during pacing, including question text, response type, and ordering.
features.aiInsights.faq.q2.question: Does AI Insights call third-party model providers?
features.aiInsights.faq.q2.answer: No. Question generation and theme clustering run on Cloudflare Workers AI inside the same edge network that serves the session — there are no calls to OpenAI, Anthropic, or other third-party LLMs.
features.aiInsights.faq.q3.question: Can I compare themes across sessions?
features.aiInsights.faq.q3.answer: Yes. Theme groupings persist with the session record so recurring patterns surface across HR pulses, retros, and recurring workshops.
features.aiInsights.bottomCta.heading: Stop staring at a blank question list.
features.aiInsights.bottomCta.subheading: Let AI draft it. You refine it. Your team answers it.
features.livePolling.seo.title: Live polling | Real-time polls, rankings, consent rounds
features.livePolling.seo.description: Run polls, rankings, consent checks, and open questions inside one session — with low latency for in-room and remote participants on any device.
features.livePolling.headline: Live polling that moves at the speed of your room.
features.livePolling.subheadline: Multiple choice, scales, rankings, open questions, and consent votes — all updating in real time on every participant's device and your presenter screen simultaneously.
features.livePolling.howItWorks.heading: From question to result in under 10 seconds
features.livePolling.howItWorks.step1.de: Participants join in the browser on any device. No app install, no account creation, no waiting room.
features.livePolling.howItWorks.step2.de: Hosts reveal, pause, and close questions based on room dynamics. Participants only see what the host has opened.
features.livePolling.howItWorks.step3.de: Live-updating visuals are sized for the room and the projector. Hosts can switch between facilitator view and audience view.
features.livePolling.outcomes.heading: Why teams switch to Qesto
features.livePolling.outcomes.item1.desc: Polls, rankings, consent rounds, and open responses live in the same session — no stitching.
features.livePolling.outcomes.item2.desc: Cloudflare's edge network keeps interaction latency consistent for distributed audiences.
features.livePolling.outcomes.item3.desc: Participants join from any modern browser — no install reduces drop-off in the first minute.
features.livePolling.proof.heading: Delivery numbers that matter live
features.livePolling.proof.metric1.label: Typical interaction latency
features.livePolling.proof.metric1.note: Measured for participants on the same continent as the edge node.
features.livePolling.proof.metric2.label: For the entire session
features.livePolling.proof.metric2.note: Hosts run multiple formats without re-prompting participants to rejoin.
features.livePolling.proof.metric3.label: On the participant side
features.livePolling.proof.metric3.note: Browsers join with a code; no native app or extension required.
features.livePolling.related.heading: See live polling in action
features.livePolling.related.link1.label: Qesto for Events
features.livePolling.related.link1.desc: Drive ranked Q&A and pulse checks across keynote and breakout.
features.livePolling.related.link2.label: Qesto for HR
features.livePolling.related.link2.desc: Run pulse sessions with anonymity controls participants can see.
features.livePolling.related.link3.label: Use case: Team Meetings
features.livePolling.related.link3.desc: Replace circular debate with consent rounds and ranked agendas.
features.livePolling.faq.heading: Common live polling questions
features.livePolling.faq.q1.question: Do participants need an app?
features.livePolling.faq.q1.answer: No. Participants join in the browser using a short code on any modern device — phone, laptop, tablet, or kiosk.
features.livePolling.faq.q2.question: Can we mix rankings and open questions in one session?
features.livePolling.faq.q2.answer: Yes. A single session can combine polls, rankings, consent rounds, and open responses, sequenced by the host.
features.livePolling.faq.q3.question: Can hosts control the pace?
features.livePolling.faq.q3.answer: Yes. Hosts reveal, pause, and close each question based on room energy. Participants only see questions the host has opened.
features.livePolling.bottomCta.heading: Your audience is waiting to be heard.
features.livePolling.bottomCta.subheadin: No app install. No waiting. Just join and vote.
features.privacy.seo.title: Privacy by default | Edge AI, anonymity, consent logs
features.privacy.seo.description: Run sensitive sessions with edge AI inference, per-session anonymity modes, and time-stamped consent logs your DPO can audit.
features.privacy.headline: Your team's responses stay yours — always.
features.privacy.subheadline: Anonymity modes, GDPR consent logging, and on-device AI inference mean participants can speak honestly and you can prove it. No third-party model providers ever see your data.
features.privacy.howItWorks.heading: Privacy is architecture, not a checkbox
features.privacy.howItWorks.step1.title: Inference runs at the edge
features.privacy.howItWorks.step1.desc: Workers AI on Cloudflare runs theme detection inside the same network boundary that serves your session — no third-party model providers.
features.privacy.howItWorks.step2.title: Pick an anonymity mode per session
features.privacy.howItWorks.step2.desc: Choose full anonymity, cohort grouping, or identified responses based on the question's sensitivity. The chosen mode is shown to participants before they answer.
features.privacy.howItWorks.step3.title: Consent is logged, not assumed
features.privacy.howItWorks.step3.desc: Every consent decision becomes a time-stamped artifact you can export for DSAR responses, governance reviews, or internal audit.
features.privacy.outcomes.heading: What privacy-first means in practice
features.privacy.outcomes.item1.desc: Open-text responses do not leave Cloudflare's edge for inference.
features.privacy.outcomes.item2.desc: Time-stamped per-participant records suitable for DSAR and audit response.
features.privacy.outcomes.item3.desc: Participants see the active mode before they respond, which lifts response honesty.
features.privacy.proof.heading: Outcomes privacy-first teams report
features.privacy.proof.metric1.label: Participant hesitation
features.privacy.proof.metric1.note: When the active anonymity mode is shown in the participant view.
features.privacy.proof.metric2.label: Consent log format
features.privacy.proof.metric2.note: Exportable as JSON or CSV with participant-scoped timestamps.
features.privacy.proof.metric3.label: Where AI runs
features.privacy.proof.metric3.note: Workers AI inference in the same network as your session traffic.
features.privacy.related.heading: Privacy matters most here
features.privacy.related.link1.label: Qesto for Consulting
features.privacy.related.link1.desc: Run sensitive client workshops with controls participants can see.
features.privacy.related.link2.label: Qesto for HR
features.privacy.related.link2.desc: Make pulse programs trustworthy enough for honest answers.
features.privacy.related.link3.label: Qesto for Nonprofits
features.privacy.related.link3.desc: Support member governance with auditable decision evidence.
features.privacy.faq.heading: Common privacy questions
features.privacy.faq.q1.question: Can we choose anonymity per session?
features.privacy.faq.q1.answer: Yes. Each session uses one of three modes — full anonymity, cohort grouping, or identified responses — and the active mode is visible to participants before they respond.
features.privacy.faq.q2.question: Where does AI inference run?
features.privacy.faq.q2.answer: Theme detection and question generation run on Cloudflare Workers AI inside the same edge network that serves your session. Open-text responses are not routed to third-party model providers.
features.privacy.faq.q3.question: What does the consent log contain?
features.privacy.faq.q3.answer: Per-participant decisions with UTC timestamps, the prompt text shown, and the mode in effect. Exportable as JSON or CSV for DSAR responses and governance review.
features.privacy.bottomCta.heading: Honest responses start with trusted infrastructure.
features.privacy.bottomCta.subheading: Privacy is on by default — you never have to turn it on.
useCases.teamMeetings.seo.title: Use case: Team meetings | Decisions with rationale
useCases.teamMeetings.seo.description: Improve retros, planning, and decision checkpoints with consent rounds, ranked agendas, and recap exports into Slack, Notion, and Linear.
useCases.teamMeetings.headline: Meetings where everyone contributes, not just the loudest.
useCases.teamMeetings.subheadline: Replace silent nodding with structured input. Qesto makes standups, retrospectives, and decision meetings faster, more inclusive, and actually useful.
useCases.teamMeetings.imageAlt: Team collaborating around a table in a meeting
useCases.teamMeetings.painPoints.heading: What makes team meetings frustrating
Team members avoid conflict in the call and surface concerns once the work is already in flight.
useCases.teamMeetings.painPoints.time.de: Unstructured discussion swallows the time you reserved for the priority decision.
Three weeks later nobody remembers why the decision was made — only that it was.
useCases.teamMeetings.features.heading: Qesto tools for better team meetings
useCases.teamMeetings.features.pulse.des: Open with a 30-second sentiment check so blockers surface before the agenda starts.
useCases.teamMeetings.features.ranking.d: Rank topics live before discussion so the highest-impact decisions get the time they deserve.
useCases.teamMeetings.features.consent.d: Validate buy-in and capture objections before closing — making post-meeting reversals visible early.
useCases.teamMeetings.features.recap.tit: Recap export to Slack, Notion, Linear
useCases.teamMeetings.features.recap.des: Send structured outcomes (decision, rationale, dissent) into the surface your team already works in.
useCases.teamMeetings.proof.heading: Meeting metrics teams improve
useCases.teamMeetings.proof.metric1.note: Illustrative target when ranked agendas and consent rounds are used each week.
useCases.teamMeetings.proof.metric2.note: Illustrative target from teams that rotate pulse checks and anonymous input.
useCases.teamMeetings.proof.metric3.note: Outcome and reasoning stay attached to the session record.
We spend less time debating because dissent is visible before we close the decision — not after the work has shipped.
useCases.teamMeetings.scenarios.heading: Meeting formats that work well with Qesto
useCases.teamMeetings.scenarios.retro.de: Collect candid input ahead of the call and discuss clustered themes instead of free-form debate.
useCases.teamMeetings.scenarios.sprint.d: Rank backlog candidates before the planning conversation so the team debates priorities, not the list.
useCases.teamMeetings.scenarios.decision: Run consent rounds before finalizing impactful changes — the rationale lives with the session record.
useCases.teamMeetings.related.heading: Related pages
useCases.teamMeetings.related.link1.desc: Use polls, rankings, and consent rounds inside one session.
useCases.teamMeetings.related.link2.desc: Enable safer participation for sensitive team topics.
useCases.teamMeetings.related.link3.desc: Scale team-level listening into org-wide pulse loops.
useCases.teamMeetings.faq.heading: Common team-meeting questions
useCases.teamMeetings.faq.q1.question: Can we use this in short standups?
useCases.teamMeetings.faq.q1.answer: Yes. A session can be a single 30-second pulse — the workflow scales down without losing the recap.
useCases.teamMeetings.faq.q2.question: Will it slow our agenda down?
useCases.teamMeetings.faq.q2.answer: Usually the opposite. Teams report shorter cycles because circular debate is cut by ranked agendas and consent rounds.
useCases.teamMeetings.faq.q3.question: Can we share outcomes after every meeting?
useCases.teamMeetings.faq.q3.answer: Yes. Recaps export to Slack, Notion, and Linear with the decision and the rationale attached.
useCases.teamMeetings.bottomCta.heading: Turn your next meeting into a real conversation.
useCases.teamMeetings.bottomCta.subheadi: Join codes work on any device — no app install for participants.
useCases.workshops.seo.title: Use case: Workshops | Hybrid facilitation craft
useCases.workshops.seo.description: Facilitate ideation and prioritization workshops with parallel idea capture, AI affinity clustering, and outputs that survive the handoff.
useCases.workshops.headline: Facilitate without the sticky notes.
useCases.workshops.subheadline: Real-time structured input replaces physical props and messy whiteboards. Run ideation, prioritisation, and consent rounds from a single session link — and leave with exportable results.
useCases.workshops.imageAlt: Workshop participants collaborating on a shared activity
useCases.workshops.painPoints.heading: What slows workshops down
useCases.workshops.painPoints.props.desc: Sticky notes, markers, and the photo phase eat the convergent half of the workshop where decisions actually happen.
useCases.workshops.painPoints.distribute: Hybrid puts remote in second class
In-room participants dominate physical-first formats while remote attendees watch the back of someone's head.
useCases.workshops.painPoints.results.de: Photo-based outputs are unsearchable, untransferable, and impossible to operationalize for the team that has to execute.
useCases.workshops.features.heading: One session link for every workshop format
useCases.workshops.features.ideas.title: Parallel idea capture
useCases.workshops.features.ideas.desc: Every participant contributes at the same time — no turn-taking, no facilitator bottleneck.
useCases.workshops.features.voting.title: Live prioritization
useCases.workshops.features.voting.desc: Use ranking to converge on the highest-value options with the math visible to the room.
useCases.workshops.features.ai.title: AI affinity clustering
useCases.workshops.features.ai.desc: Open responses are grouped into themes by edge AI so the convergent phase starts with structure, not a wall of text.
useCases.workshops.features.results.desc: Export workshop outputs as structured artifacts the execution team can read on Monday morning.
useCases.workshops.proof.heading: Workshop KPIs facilitators improve
useCases.workshops.proof.metric1.label: Convergence time
useCases.workshops.proof.metric1.note: Illustrative target when AI clustering replaces manual affinity sorting.
useCases.workshops.proof.metric2.label: Shared interaction flow
useCases.workshops.proof.metric2.note: Remote and in-room participants use the same join flow and facilitation sequence.
useCases.workshops.proof.metric3.label: Structured handoff
useCases.workshops.proof.metric3.note: Workshop outputs are export-ready for Monday execution planning.
useCases.workshops.proof.testimonial.quo: We end workshops with decisions and structured outputs the execution team actually reads, not a folder of whiteboard photos.
useCases.workshops.scenarios.heading: Workshop formats that use Qesto
useCases.workshops.scenarios.design.desc: Move from divergent ideation to convergent ranking inside one session — no tool switch.
useCases.workshops.scenarios.values.desc: Capture individual input anonymously and let the group prioritize shared principles transparently.
useCases.workshops.scenarios.hybrid.desc: Keep remote and in-room voices equally weighted in the live decision moments.
useCases.workshops.related.heading: Related pages
useCases.workshops.related.link1.label: Qesto for Consulting
useCases.workshops.related.link1.desc: See the commercial-deliverable view of the same workflow.
useCases.workshops.related.link2.label: Feature: AI Insights
useCases.workshops.related.link2.desc: Edge-AI clustering for the affinity phase.
useCases.workshops.related.link3.label: Feature: Live Polling
useCases.workshops.related.link3.desc: Ranking and consent rounds for the convergent phase.
useCases.workshops.faq.heading: Common workshop questions
useCases.workshops.faq.q1.question: Can we run this without physical sticky notes?
useCases.workshops.faq.q1.answer: Yes. The flow is fully digital from divergent ideation through convergent ranking — and the outputs survive the handoff.
useCases.workshops.faq.q2.question: Does it support hybrid facilitation?
useCases.workshops.faq.q2.answer: Yes. In-room and remote participants share the same join code, the same question flow, and the same view.
useCases.workshops.faq.q3.question: Can we export workshop outcomes quickly?
useCases.workshops.faq.q3.answer: Yes. Structured outputs are available immediately and can be shared before participants leave the room.
useCases.workshops.bottomCta.heading: Great facilitation leaves evidence.
useCases.workshops.bottomCta.subheading: Qesto turns workshop conversations into structured, shareable results.
useCases.training.seo.title: Use case: Training | Live formative checks
useCases.training.seo.description: Run formative checks, confidence scales, and reflection prompts with real-time feedback — Kirkpatrick L1 and L2 evidence inside the session.
useCases.training.headline: Check understanding while you still have the room.
useCases.training.subheadline: Run formative knowledge checks, confidence surveys, and reflection questions mid-session. Find out what participants actually understood — not what they said they understood.
useCases.training.imageAlt: Trainer leading an interactive learning session
useCases.training.painPoints.heading: Why training assessments usually fail
useCases.training.painPoints.passive.des: Cameras stay on and heads stay up while the actual concept lands with one third of the cohort.
useCases.training.painPoints.silence.des: Public-question formats favour the confident learners and leave gaps invisible until the assessment.
useCases.training.painPoints.surveys.des: Post-session surveys land after the room has emptied and the trainer has moved on to the next cohort.
useCases.training.features.heading: Qesto tools for training sessions
useCases.training.features.formative.des: Run quick comprehension checks at module boundaries and re-teach where the data points.
useCases.training.features.confidence.de: Measure learner self-rated readiness before progressing — surfaces quiet uncertainty without public exposure.
useCases.training.features.reflection.de: Capture learner takeaways and unresolved questions in real time, scoped to the cohort.
useCases.training.features.ai.title: AI summary support
useCases.training.features.ai.desc: Edge-AI clusters open reflections so trainers see the misconception pattern without manual coding.
useCases.training.proof.heading: Learning outcomes L&D teams track
useCases.training.proof.metric1.label: Comprehension visibility
useCases.training.proof.metric1.note: Trainers spot gaps before the session closes — Kirkpatrick L2 inside the room.
useCases.training.proof.metric2.label: Instructional adjustment lag
useCases.training.proof.metric2.note: Confidence checks surface misunderstanding early enough to re-teach before the next module.
useCases.training.proof.metric3.label: L&D evidence
useCases.training.proof.metric3.note: Structured outputs feed reporting at L1 (reaction) and L2 (learning).
useCases.training.proof.testimonial.quot: We can detect misunderstanding mid-session and re-teach the same hour — the smile sheet becomes confirmatory, not corrective.
useCases.training.scenarios.heading: Training scenarios where Qesto helps
useCases.training.scenarios.midModule.de: Validate comprehension before introducing more advanced material — re-teach where the data shows it.
useCases.training.scenarios.reflection.d: Capture takeaways and unresolved questions before learners log off — and feed the next session brief.
useCases.training.scenarios.baseline.des: Pre/post comparisons across an onboarding cohort show confidence and knowledge shifts over the program.
useCases.training.related.heading: Related pages
useCases.training.related.link1.label: Feature: Live Polling
useCases.training.related.link1.desc: Deploy quick checks and confidence scales inside the module.
useCases.training.related.link2.label: Feature: AI Insights
useCases.training.related.link2.desc: Cluster open learner reflections without manual coding.
useCases.training.related.link3.label: Qesto for Events
useCases.training.related.link3.desc: Apply interactive learning patterns at large-audience scale.
useCases.training.faq.heading: Common training questions
useCases.training.faq.q1.question: Can learners join without creating accounts?
useCases.training.faq.q1.answer: Yes. Learners join with a session code in the browser — no install, no account, no waiting room.
useCases.training.faq.q2.question: Can trainers run checks during modules?
useCases.training.faq.q2.answer: Yes. Trainers control timing — reveal, pause, and close each check based on the room's pace.
useCases.training.faq.q3.question: Can we use outcomes in L&D reporting?
useCases.training.faq.q3.answer: Yes. Structured outputs feed Kirkpatrick L1 (reaction) and L2 (learning) summaries for program review.
useCases.training.bottomCta.heading: Train smarter — check understanding while it matters.
useCases.training.bottomCta.subheading: No app required. Participants join by code on any device.
